title: cornus rosea
intro: Cornus rosea, also known as red osier dogwood, is a deciduous shrub native to North America, characterized by its red or yellowish stems and white or pink flowers. The medicinal properties of Cornus rosea include its use as a diuretic and an anti-inflammatory agent, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Cornus rosea include its ability to support heart health, lower blood pressure, and alleviate symptoms of menopause, as well as its potential anti-cancer properties. Cornus rosea is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and can also be used in combination with other herbs to create herbal preparations such as infusions and salves.
